# Technical Plan Requirements Quality Checklist: WikiStream MVP Vertical Slice

**Purpose**: Validate whether the technical plan and supporting design artifacts are clear, complete, consistent, and ready for task generation.
**Created**: 2026-06-05
**Feature**: [`spec.md`](../spec.md), [`plan.md`](../plan.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements. It tests the quality of written requirements/design intent, not implementation behavior.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are all spec-required MVP capabilities mapped to concrete technical plan components: live ingestion, Redpanda topic, processor, storage, dashboard, replay, data-quality counts, and documentation? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001..FR-015, Plan §Summary]
- [x] CHK002 Are the boundaries between ingestor, processor, shared core package, analytical storage, and dashboard responsibilities explicitly defined enough for task generation? [Completeness, Plan §Project Structure]
- [x] CHK003 Are local orchestration requirements complete for all planned services and dependencies, including Redpanda and dashboard access? [Completeness, Plan §Technical Context]
  - Resolved: Plan and topic contract now document Redpanda addresses, snapshot volume/read-write roles, service dependencies, dashboard empty-state tolerance, and live/replay mode wiring.
- [x] CHK004 Are storage requirements complete for normalized events, activity metrics, bot spike signals, and data-quality counts? [Completeness, Spec §Key Entities, Plan §Technical Context]
- [x] CHK005 Are documentation requirements represented in the plan beyond README mention, including purpose, problem framing, usage, architecture, replay mode, data quality, responsible limits, and known limitations? [Completeness, Spec §FR-015]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Is the planned Parquet/DuckDB storage pattern described with enough precision to distinguish processor write responsibilities from dashboard read/query responsibilities? [Clarity, Plan §Technical Context, Plan §Phase 0 Research Summary]
- [x] CHK007 Is the phrase “representative RecentChanges local throughput” quantified or bounded enough to guide implementation choices? [Ambiguity, Plan §Performance Goals]
  - Resolved: Plan now defines a 300 events/minute MVP processing target, bounded memory expectation, and summarization behavior under higher volume.
- [x] CHK008 Is the chosen Python SSE client dependency clarified enough to avoid competing implementation paths between `requests`, `httpx`, or another SSE client? [Ambiguity, Plan §Primary Dependencies]
  - Resolved: Plan and research now choose `httpx` plus `httpx-sse` for EventStreams ingestion.
- [x] CHK009 Are the terms “current window,” “recent baseline,” “threshold ratio,” and “minimum current events” specified clearly enough for the bot spike detector? [Clarity, Spec §FR-006, Data Model §BotSpikeSignal]
  - Resolved: Plan, data model, research, quickstart, and dashboard contract now define 5-minute current window, 30-minute baseline, 20-event minimum, 3.0x threshold, and zero-baseline handling.
- [x] CHK010 Is the fallback from event-time to processing-time explicitly defined enough to avoid inconsistent windowing behavior? [Clarity, Data Model §NormalizedRecentChangeEvent]
  - Resolved: Data model now specifies event-time use when parseable and not more than 10 minutes ahead of observed time, with observed-time fallback otherwise.
- [x] CHK011 Are “malformed/rejected” versus “missing-field” classifications defined with concrete field-level examples? [Clarity, Spec §FR-011, Data Model §DataQualityCount]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK012 Is the plan’s “Parquet snapshots plus DuckDB reads” decision consistent across the plan, research, data model, contracts, and quickstart? [Consistency, Plan §Technical Context, Research §Analytical storage]
- [x] CHK013 Are replay-mode requirements consistent between the spec, replay contract, data model, and quickstart regarding labels, expected spike, and freshness treatment? [Consistency, Spec §FR-009..FR-010, Replay Contract, Quickstart §Replay mode validation]
- [x] CHK014 Are responsible-observability wording constraints consistent between the constitution, spec, plan, dashboard contract, and bot spike data model? [Consistency, Constitution §II, Spec §FR-008, Dashboard Contract §Domain-level bot spike signal]
- [x] CHK015 Are scope exclusions consistent across spec, plan, and constitution, especially no cloud, no Kubernetes, no complex ML, no historical backfill, and no writing to Wikimedia? [Consistency, Constitution §Project Constraints, Spec §FR-014, Plan §Constraints]
- [x] CHK016 Is the planned project structure consistent with the stated goal of a narrow vertical slice rather than introducing unneeded services or abstractions? [Consistency, Constitution §V, Plan §Project Structure]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK017 Are the 5-minute live startup, 2-minute replay startup, and 60-second freshness thresholds reflected in the plan and quickstart with measurable acceptance language? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-001..SC-005, Quickstart]
- [x] CHK018 Is the 95% handling target for intentionally malformed or missing-field sample events supported by clear validation data requirements? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-006, Replay Contract §Required sample coverage]
- [x] CHK019 Can “dashboard metrics update over time” be objectively assessed from the written requirements, or is refresh interval behavior still under-specified? [Measurability, Spec §User Story 1]
  - Resolved: Plan, quickstart, and dashboard contract now define a 15-second default dashboard refresh/query cadence.
- [x] CHK020 Are bot spike outputs measurable enough to determine whether the numeric/categorical magnitude requirement is satisfied? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004, Data Model §BotSpikeSignal]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK021 Are primary live-mode, replay-mode, no-spike, stale-data, malformed-event, and missing-field scenarios all covered by spec, contracts, or quickstart? [Coverage, Spec §User Scenarios & Testing, Quickstart]
- [x] CHK022 Are exception scenarios for Wikimedia stream unavailability or disconnects specified beyond dashboard stale/no-data communication? [Coverage, Gap, Spec §Edge Cases]
  - Resolved: Plan and quickstart now require capped exponential backoff and structured reconnect logs for EventStreams disconnects/transient errors.
- [x] CHK023 Are recovery requirements documented for restarting services after broker, ingestor, or processor interruption? [Gap, Recovery Flow]
  - Resolved: Plan and quickstart now document restart-safe MVP behavior using Redpanda retention, Kafka offsets, idempotent event ids/deterministic hashes, dashboard empty states, and rerunnable replay mode.
- [x] CHK024 Are empty snapshot or missing snapshot scenarios specified for the dashboard without relying on implementation assumptions? [Coverage, Dashboard Contract §Empty/error states]
- [x] CHK025 Are high-volume stream scenarios specified in terms of summarization boundaries, retention, or snapshot compaction expectations? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Plan §Performance Goals]
  - Resolved: Plan and topic contract now state bounded snapshot retention of approximately 6 hours for generated live snapshots and summarize rather than list all events.

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK026 Are external Wikimedia assumptions documented with current-source verification expectations, including EventStreams behavior, schema fields, and read-only etiquette? [Dependency, Constitution §Project Constraints, Research §Wikimedia RecentChanges ingestion]
- [x] CHK027 Are local dependency assumptions documented sufficiently for users, including Docker Compose, image builds, ports, volumes, and no credential requirements? [Dependency, Plan §Technical Context, Quickstart §Prerequisites]
  - Resolved: Plan, quickstart, and topic contract now document expected ports, shared snapshot volume behavior, broker readiness expectations, and no-credential assumptions.
- [x] CHK028 Are Kafka/Redpanda topic contracts complete enough to support producer/consumer task creation without hidden assumptions? [Dependency, Topic Contracts §Redpanda topics]
- [x] CHK029 Are Python package choices explicit enough to prevent task ambiguity around dependency files and shared-package installation? [Dependency, Plan §Primary Dependencies, Plan §Project Structure]
  - Resolved: Plan now specifies a root `pyproject.toml`, editable installs for the shared package, service entry points, and `httpx`/`httpx-sse` for SSE.

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK030 Are observability/logging requirements specified with enough structure to guide service-level logging without adding a logging stack? [Non-Functional, Research §Structured logging]
- [x] CHK031 Are local reproducibility requirements complete enough to ensure replay mode works without live internet access after dependencies are available? [Non-Functional, Spec §FR-009, Quickstart §Replay mode validation]
- [x] CHK032 Are data retention, snapshot file growth, and generated-file cleanup requirements intentionally excluded or still missing for local MVP use? [Gap, Non-Functional]
  - Resolved: Plan, topic contract, and quickstart now define approximate 6-hour live snapshot retention and `rm -rf data/snapshots/*` cleanup guidance.
- [x] CHK033 Are privacy/responsible-publication requirements clear enough for local dashboard account-label context and future public artifacts? [Non-Functional, Constitution §II, Spec §FR-012]

## Traceability and Task Readiness

- [x] CHK034 Is every planned implementation area traceable to at least one spec requirement, user story, contract, or constitution principle? [Traceability, Plan §Post-Design Constitution Check]
- [x] CHK035 Are design artifacts complete enough for `/speckit.tasks` to create independently testable user-story phases without inventing missing requirements? [Task Readiness, Plan §Phase 1 Design Summary]
  - Resolved: Plan/design docs now define spike defaults, refresh cadence, reconnect behavior, restart expectations, and retention/cleanup guidance.
- [x] CHK036 Are unresolved ambiguities explicitly marked as gaps or assumptions before task generation rather than hidden in implementation choices? [Ambiguity, Task Readiness]
  - Resolved: Previously identified ambiguities are now resolved in plan, research, data model, contracts, and quickstart before task generation.

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
